---
title: Initialize configuration
description: How to configure your media library with the `astro.config.mjs` file.
---

import { Steps } from "@astrojs/starlight/components";

The primary configuration file for your LightNet project is `astro.config.mjs`.
This file defines global configuration for your media library, such as the title, languages, base URL, and more.

Here is a minimal version of an `astro.config.mjs` file for a LightNet site:

```js title="astro.config.mjs"
import lightnet from "lightnet";
import { defineConfig } from "astro/config";

export default defineConfig({
  site: "https://yourdomain.com",
  integrations: [
    lightnet({
      title: {
        en: "My Library",
      },
      languages: [
        {
          code: "en",
          label: {
            en: "English",
          },
          isDefaultSiteLanguage: true,
        },
      ],
    }),
  ],
});
```

Customize the settings to match your needs.

<Steps>
1. **Site** -
   The `site` property defines the base URL for your website. Update this to reflect your deployment URL.
   ```js
   site: "https://yourdomain.com"
   ```

2. **Title** -
The `title` property sets the site title, which appears in the browser tab and header bar.
   Provide a locale map keyed by your configured site languages.
   ```js
   title: { en: "My Library" }
   ```

3. **Languages** -
   The `languages` section defines the languages known to your site. Use `isDefaultSiteLanguage` and `isSiteLanguage` to control which locales are available in the site UI. Refer to the [Internationalization Guide](/build/i18n/fundamentals) for more 
   details on managing locales and fallbacks.

   Each language also includes its display label as a locale map. For example:
   ```json
   {
     "code": "en",
     "label": {
       "en": "English"
     },
     "isDefaultSiteLanguage": true
   }
   ```
</Steps>

More configuration options are available. Use the other guides in this chapter to customize your site further.
